briefing.headline: Rig up a Walking Topwater — steady summer approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Walking Topwater is a strong choice for summer with stained water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: Used an X-Rap Prop and handcarved prop bait in bluegill patterns around bluegill beds; when a bass dogs the topwater darting back and forth without striking, pause the bait for as long as 6 seconds until the fish inhales it.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Used an X-Rap Prop and handcarved prop bait in bluegill patterns around bluegill beds; when a bass dogs the topwater darting back and forth without striking, pause the bait for as long as 6 s
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, hump, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use monofilament (Sufix Siege) for topwater fishing — the stretch prevents jerking the bait away from the fish on the hookset. Pull into the fish rather than jerking, letting the sharp VMC ho
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, hump, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Hot water dog days: bait and bass suspend off structure over deep points and humps. Clear water makes them selective and easily spooked — odds of catching several from one spot are minimal. Hit a lot of spots, catch a few, move on. Go back t...
Palaniuk: 'Walk the frog on top of thick grass to get bass to blow through it, or fish it on edges and points of the grass. Walk it 90% of the time like a Zara Spook'
Hackney: 'Bluegill and bream are the bait bass feed on most at this time of year. A bream-colored topwater looks like a bream jumping, skipping, and moving on the surface.'
Wheeler: When fishing ledges, keep baits on bottom but often burn them fast then kill the retrieve to trigger reaction strikes — this produced key fish at Chickamauga

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable summer patterns are predictable. Dawn/dusk fish are shallow (3-8ft) near cover. Midday fish pull to offshore structure at 12-25ft — ledges, humps, brush piles, and creek channel bends.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are blowing up but not connecting, pause 3-5 seconds after each strike before resuming
